chapter_title.ar: باب صَلَاةِ الشَّيْخِ الْكَبِيرِ وَ الْمَرِيضِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 64 – The Salāt of an elderly man and the sick

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/333)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/333)

parts.text: جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ أَنَّهُ سَأَلَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا حَدُّ الْمَرِيضِ الَّذِي يُصَلِّي قَاعِداً

text: إِنَّ الرَّجُلَ لَيُوعَكُ وَ يَحْرَجُ وَ لَكِنَّهُ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِنَفْسِهِ وَ لَكِنْ إِذَا قَوِيَ فَلْيَقُمْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr,

(It has been narrated) from Jameel Bin Darraj who asked Abu Abdullahasws, ‘What is a limit for the sick by which he can pray Salāt seated?’ So heasws said: ‘If the man is unwell and is hindered. But, he is more knowing of himself. However, if he is strong enough, so let him stand’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘ Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who suffers from an illness. In what condition he is allowed to perform Salah (prayer) in a sitting position? He (the Imam) said, ‘A man is unwell and experiences anguish; however, he knows better about himself. When he is able he must stand up.’”
